topic: Counter-narrative stress test (Phase 2 rerun) — re-evaluate the dome-defender attack surface after 6 weeks of substantive content changes since the April 2026 c1 pass
what_changed_since_c1: eg-falsifiability section added to Evaluation Guide (docs/index.html:664): 'What Would Change Our Verdict' with 4 explicit conditions + eclipse pre-commitment. Closes c1 holes 1 and 5.

ds-falsifiability-module added to Overview tab (docs/index.html:433-456): 4-condition status table with '0/70 met • 0/70 met • 1 partial • 0/70 met' badges and a 'Full criteria →' jump link to eg-falsifiability.

eg-verdicts 'Verdict Assignment Criteria' section added (docs/index.html:633): primary-issue rule, Misleading/Std-Model decision boundary, and explicit 'On the absence of a Confirmed category' paragraph. Closes c1 hole 2 framing and partially preempts the 'no success category' critique.

Std Model Explains framing softened: docs/index.html:621 adds 'this verdict does not mean the observation is wrong' paragraph acknowledging the dome correctly identifies real phenomena. Closes c1 hole 2.
Honest-accuracy math card added (docs/index.html accuracy-math-section): 89.7% vs claimed 94.5%, with breakdown of acknowledged + silent failures. Adds a positive-framing element to the overview.

WIN count grew 67→70 dome-claimed (72 catalog entries including 2 'b'-suffix sub-claims); 3 new WINs added (WIN-068 Eclipse magnetic ensemble, WIN-069 Australia road scaffold, WIN-070 Forensic Zoom L1A induction).
Stance statement opener added to Overview (docs/index.html:341): 'The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology.'
Five-Questions framework section ds-evaluate-preface added to Overview (docs/index.html:476): five questions framed as click-throughs into the verdict sections.

Six holistic c2/c3 reruns this commission cycle (HOL-NARRATIVE, HOL-COMPLETENESS, HOL-CROSSREF, HOL-TAXONOMY, HOL-HIERARCHY, HOL-TONE) generated 35 fresh holes — most are surface-level, but several intersect the stress-test surface (HOL-TAXONOMY confirms c1 taxonomy hole architecturally preempted; HOL-NARRATIVE flagged stance-statement and 70-vs-72 count drift).

c1_disposition.c1_hole: No explicit success category — what would change our verdict?
c1_disposition.evidence: docs/index.html:664 eg-falsifiability section with 4 named conditions; docs/index.html:433 ds-falsifiability-module on Overview; docs/index.html:660 'On the absence of a Confirmed category' paragraph in eg-verdicts; closed via ISS HOL-STRESSTEST chain.

c1_disposition.c1_hole: Std Model Explains framed as undifferentiated negative
c1_disposition.evidence: docs/index.html:621 explanatory paragraph in eg-evaluate plus Verdict Assignment Criteria primary-issue rule (docs/index.html:633).

c1_disposition.c1_hole: Medicine analogy assumes reader trusts Theory A (standard physics)
c1_disposition.evidence: Analogy still present at docs/index.html:620 in original form; no second analogy added for anti-establishment readers. The c1 recommendation was to add a curve-fit/post-hoc framing that works without trusting Theory A — not done. Acknowledged Failures + Silent Failures counter-framing in scorecard (89.7% honest accuracy) does some of this work indirectly.

c1_disposition.c1_hole: No asymmetric burden of proof acknowledgment
c1_disposition.evidence: docs/index.html:4899 ('globe has 400 years of refinement; dome was created in 2024') acknowledges asymmetry inside Part 8 but not in the Evaluation Guide preface where the c1 rec placed it; a fence-sitting reader who never reaches Part 8 will not encounter it.

docs/index.html:670 explicit pre-commitment paragraph: 5-20 nT Chapman range, -17 to -21 nT dome range, 1 nT discriminating window, 'we state this now, before the eclipse, so there is no ambiguity afterward.'

c1_disposition.c1_hole: Scorecard has no positive framing acknowledging real observations
c1_disposition.evidence: docs/index.html:703 sublabel 'None produces a dome-derived prediction that both differs from standard physics and is confirmed by measurement. What would change this?'; docs/index.html:621 'the dome model correctly identifies a real phenomenon' framing.

the_single_best_rebuttal_c2.title: The Falsifiability Module Is a Tautological Restatement of the Verdict Architecture
the_single_best_rebuttal_c2.dome_defender_argument: You added a 'What Would Change Our Verdict' module on the overview, with 4 conditions and 4 status badges. Let us read those conditions carefully. Condition 1: 'Derive a prediction from dome geometry that differs from standard physics, then confirm it.' Condition 3: 'Derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry without using globe radius.' Condition 4: 'Sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs.' These three conditions are exactly what your 'Self-Contradicted' and 'Misleading' verdicts already negate by stipulation. You have classified WIN-001 (Tesla 11.79 Hz) as Refuted by Data — but in your own analysis, that means we DID derive a value (11.79 Hz) different from standard physics (7.83 Hz). Your verdict says the data refutes the dome value, not that we failed to make a discriminating prediction. By writing Condition 1 as 'differs from standard physics AND confirmed by measurement' you have made it logically impossible for any WIN in your Refuted-by-Data column to qualify — even though those are precisely the WINs that meet the first half (differs from standard physics). The 4 conditions are not independent tests; they are restated versions of the existing six failure categories with the success cases extracted. The four '0/70 met' badges on the overview are therefore not falsifiability evidence — they are the existing review compressed into status chrome. You have stated four conditions whose denominator (70) and numerator (0) are both fixed by your own prior verdicts. The eclipse '1 partial' badge is the only condition whose status could actually change on a non-verdict-revision schedule, and even there your eclipse paragraph concedes the discriminating window is approximately 1 nT — which means the eclipse cannot satisfy Condition 1 either, since 1 nT is by your own admission within the standard-physics envelope.
why_this_is_effective: It attacks the c1 fix specifically, not the original framework — the dome defender concedes the c1 critique was answered while showing the answer is structurally hollow
It uses the review's own verdict labels and explicit text as evidence rather than asserting bias — the argument requires the dome defender to do less interpretive work than the taxonomy critique did

It produces a concrete actionable counter: the falsifiability module would be stronger if at least one condition were operationalized as a measurement of an in-flight prediction (eclipse comes close, but the 1-nT-window framing undercuts it) rather than restating the existing verdict criteria

It exploits the visual rhetoric of the new module: '0/70 0/70 1-partial 0/70' reads as exactly the same indictment the c1 review was supposed to soften — three more zeros in big-number formatting next to the existing scorecard 0
It does not require the reader to disagree with any individual WIN verdict — accepting all of them, the falsifiability module still adds no independent test
the_single_best_rebuttal_c2.strength_rating: 7.5/10 — Rhetorically narrower than the c1 taxonomy critique (which was 8.5) but structurally more accurate because it targets a specific UI element introduced in response to c1. Loses points because the eclipse pre-commitment is a genuine future test with a stated condition that COULD be met (just barely), so the module is not pure tautology. A careful reader can defend Condition 1 by noting that 'differs from standard physics AND is confirmed' is exactly the standard scientific bar, but the dome defender's framing will be persuasive to a casual reader who notices the 0/70 0/70 0/70 zero stack.

partial_preemptions.location: docs/index.html:660 ('On the absence of a Confirmed category' paragraph)
partial_preemptions.text_summary: 'If we found a WIN where the dome's prediction — derived from dome geometry, not adopted from standard physics — preceded and matched an observation that standard physics could not explain, we would add such a category. We have not found one. The category's absence reflects the evidence, not an assumption.'
partial_preemptions.effectiveness: High for the c1 taxonomy critique; low for the c2 tautology critique. This paragraph defends against 'the framework has no success node' but does not address 'the falsifiability module restates the framework.' It promises a Confirmed category if conditions were met but does not show the conditions could be met independently of revising existing verdicts.

partial_preemptions.text_summary: 'If the dome's predictions are confirmed AND fall outside the Chapman-mechanism range — that is, if the measured values match the dome's −17 to −21 nT range but not the 5-20 nT Chapman range — we will upgrade the relevant WIN. ... The discriminating window is approximately 1 nT — a reflection of how little the dome's prediction actually differs from the standard model it claims to replace.'
partial_preemptions.effectiveness: Medium. This is the strongest single defense against the c2 critique because it operationalizes Condition 1 as a future test. But the same paragraph concedes the discriminating window is ~1 nT, which a dome defender can quote to argue 'by your own admission, Condition 1 is set so tightly that no plausible measurement could satisfy it.' The pre-commitment closes the circularity at the cost of admitting near-circularity.

partial_preemptions.text_summary: Condition 2 is 'Genuine prospective prediction' with status '1 partial • eclipse Aug 12, 2026' — the only non-zero status badge in the module.
partial_preemptions.effectiveness: Medium-Low. The '1 partial' status badge is visually outnumbered three-to-one by the '0/70 met' badges around it. A casual reader sees the module as four zeros with one tiny partial; the partial does not dominate the visual impression.

gaps_in_preemption: The review never states that the 4 falsifiability conditions are independent tests of the framework — they are stated in parallel to the verdict categories without explaining why a future WIN satisfying Condition 1 would not simply have been classified differently a priori.

The dome defender's tautology critique is enabled by the visual design of the ds-falsifiability-module: the four-badge zero stack mirrors the verdict-bar zero stack above it. A reader who skims sees the same indictment twice in succession, reinforcing rather than countering the c1 'rigged framework' optics.

The eclipse pre-commitment is the strongest defense but is buried in the long-form eg-falsifiability section, not surfaced in the overview module itself. The overview module shows '1 partial' without explaining the discriminating window or the pre-committed measurement criteria.
No worked example exists of 'what a WIN satisfying Condition 1 would look like' — the dome defender can credibly assert the conditions are unsatisfiable because no template for satisfaction is given.

secondary_rebuttals_c2.title: The Thesis Hero Overstates by Conflating Refutation with Non-Discrimination
secondary_rebuttals_c2.argument: Your overview hero text states: 'None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.' But your own verdict assignments contradict this. WIN-001 (Tesla 11.78 Hz, Refuted by Data) DID produce a dome-specific result: 11.78 Hz, distinct from standard physics' 7.83 Hz. WIN-002 (Schumann 26% aetheric damping, Self-Contradicted) DID produce a dome-specific quantitative prediction. WIN-070 (Forensic Zoom L1A induction, Refuted by Data) explicitly claims κ = 1.67 nT/µGal, a dome-specific value standard physics does not predict (standard physics predicts zero). The honest formulation is: 'None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that survives both internal-consistency and measurement checks.' Your worded hero conflates 'standard physics already predicts the same value' (Std Model Explains, 18 WINs) with 'the dome's discriminating prediction was wrong' (Refuted by Data, 12 WINs) — two structurally distinct failure modes that your own Verdict Assignment Criteria section spends ~200 words distinguishing.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.strength: 7/10 — Internal contradiction between hero rhetoric and verdict architecture, easy for a dome defender to spot and verbatim-quote. Loses points because the more careful formulation ('survives scrutiny') is one click away in the scorecard sublabel — but the hero is what readers see first.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.review_preemption: docs/index.html:421 verdict bars show 12 Refuted by Data + 20 Self-Contradicted as distinct categories. A careful reader can see the distinction in the bars below the hero, but the hero text itself does not preempt the conflation. The 'Survive Scrutiny' sublabel at docs/index.html:703 ('differs from standard physics AND is confirmed') is the more accurate framing but uses different language than the hero.

secondary_rebuttals_c2.title: The Stance Statement Violates Your Own Principle 3
secondary_rebuttals_c2.argument: Your Evaluation Guide Principle 3 (docs/index.html, eg-evaluate) explicitly states: 'Mainstream physics is not assumed correct by default.' Your overview opener at docs/index.html:341 states: 'The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology.' Principle 3 and the stance statement cannot both be true: either you don't assume mainstream physics is correct, or you state the conclusion ('world is a globe') as a starting position. A dome defender can quote both verbatim and ask the reader which the review is actually doing. The stance statement is your conclusion stated as your premise, presented above any evidence the reader will encounter.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.strength: 6.5/10 — Procedural critique against the review's own methodological commitments. Already partially flagged in HOLISTIC-HOL-NARRATIVE.c2 (2026-05-28) as moderate. Loses points because the next sentence after the stance statement ('That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make') explicitly acknowledges the framing, but the dome defender can still extract a clean Principle-3-vs-stance-statement quote pair.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.review_preemption: docs/index.html:341 second sentence ('That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does') attempts to soften the assertion. Effectiveness is moderate — the dome defender's 'Principle 3 says X, stance statement says not-X' attack quotes both verbatim and is unaffected by the softener.

secondary_rebuttals_c2.title: The 89.7% Honest-Accuracy Number Uses a Constructed Denominator
secondary_rebuttals_c2.argument: You compute honest accuracy as 70 confirmed ÷ (70 + 4 acknowledged + 4 silent) = 89.7%. The 4 'silent failures' are a denominator choice you made — specifically PRED-105/106/etc., which you classify as failures because they are past their window-close date with no posted result. But the dome's public methodology page treats unresolved predictions as pending, not failed. You have unilaterally redefined the denominator and then compared the redefined denominator to the dome's self-stated 94.5%, calling the dome's number dishonest. That is exactly the rhetorical move you criticize the dome for in other contexts: defining a metric, then claiming the other side is wrong by that metric. The 4 silent-failures choice has no operational definition that would prevent it growing to 5 or 7 or 10 next month — and each addition further degrades the dome's headline accuracy. Without a pre-registered 'what counts as silent failure' criterion, your 89.7% is no more rigorous than the dome's 94.5%.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.strength: 6/10 — Real critique of methodology symmetry. Effective because it turns the review's 'transparency' rhetoric back on itself. Weakens for any reader who reads the p3-failures section, where each silent failure is named and justified, but the overview accuracy card does not link to those justifications inline.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.review_preemption: docs/index.html accuracy-math-section second-column paragraph notes the dome's 94.5% is 'manually entered with a self-serving denominator' and links to Section 6.6 for full source-code analysis. The implicit claim that the review's denominator is by contrast NOT self-serving is not directly defended; a 'what counts as a silent failure' definition is not surfaced on the overview.

secondary_rebuttals_c2.title: Six Holistic Reruns in Six Days = Methodology Churn Optics
secondary_rebuttals_c2.argument: In the last week of May 2026 alone, your own holistic reviewer produced fresh c2/c3 reviews of HOL-NARRATIVE, HOL-COMPLETENESS, HOL-CROSSREF, HOL-TAXONOMY, HOL-HIERARCHY, and HOL-TONE — 35 fresh holes flagged in your own document. Meanwhile, our model has not been edited in 41+ days (per your own poller summary: 'Manual drought: d≈41.8 days'). Who has had to defend their methodology? The model that's stable for 6 weeks, or the review that's finding new errors in itself daily? Optics: stability vs churn.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.strength: 4/10 — Rhetorically available to a casual reader, logically backwards. Finding holes in your own work is what adversarial review is supposed to do. Loses badly for any reader who knows the curmudgeon agent's purpose. Including it for completeness — the dome defender WILL try it, and it WILL land with some non-technical audiences regardless of its logical weakness.
secondary_rebuttals_c2.review_preemption: Not preempted directly. The curmudgeon's role is documented in agent prompts but not visible to public readers. A short Overview paragraph explaining the review-as-living-document framing would neutralize this, but no such paragraph exists.

composite_dome_defense_c2.description: A coordinated c2 dome defense combines the tautology critique (primary) with the hero-text contradiction (secondary) into a single rhetorical move: 'Your review claims falsifiability but the new What-Would-Change-Our-Verdict module is a restatement of your existing verdicts in different language — and even your hero text contradicts your own verdict architecture by treating Refuted-by-Data WINs as if they had not produced dome-specific results.' This is more compact than the c1 composite defense (which combined taxonomy critique with eclipse challenge) because the eclipse is now explicitly addressed in the pre-commitment. The c2 defense focuses on the architectural follow-through gap: c1 framing fixes were applied (the eg-falsifiability section, the 'no Confirmed category' preempt) but the architecture (verdict categories, hero thesis wording) was not updated to match. The dome defender's ideal phrasing: 'You added a section that says you would change your mind, but your homepage still asserts the conclusion and your verdict categories still have no success node — you have addressed the c1 critique in the prose but not in the structure.'
composite_dome_defense_c2.review_vulnerability: The c2 vulnerability is structural lag — framing changes outpace architecture changes. The fix is to (a) revise the hero text to reflect what verdict labels actually mean ('None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that survives both internal-consistency and measurement checks'), (b) surface the eclipse pre-commitment's discriminating-window detail in the overview module rather than burying it in the long-form eg-falsifiability, and (c) consider whether a 'Genuine Prediction (pending future test)' verdict category should exist as a placeholder for in-flight predictions like the eclipse — a non-empty success-side category would defeat the tautology critique decisively.

holes_found.description: The new ds-falsifiability-module on the Overview tab (docs/index.html:433-456) is rhetorically vulnerable to the 'tautological restatement' critique. Three of the four conditions (C1 differs-from-standard-physics, C3 derive 7.83 Hz, C4 sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs) reformulate the criteria the existing Self-Contradicted and Misleading verdicts already operate on. The four-badge '0/70 met • 0/70 met • 1 partial • 0/70 met' status row visually mirrors the verdict-bar zeros above it, reinforcing rather than countering the c1 'rigged framework' optics. The condition-2 eclipse pre-commitment is the only operationally-falsifiable condition but is buried in the long-form eg-falsifiability section rather than surfaced in the module.
holes_found.recommendation: Restructure the module to (a) explicitly name which conditions are operationalizable as future tests (currently only C2 eclipse, and arguably C3 if a new derivation paper emerged); (b) move the eclipse pre-commitment paragraph's discriminating-window detail INTO the C2 status row's expanded tooltip or sublabel; (c) consider adding a fifth, more open-ended condition ('any other genuinely discriminating dome-derived prediction not anticipated by the four conditions above') to break the appearance of fixed-frame restatement. Alternatively: relabel the module heading from 'What Would Change Our Verdict' to 'Conditions We Have Pre-Committed To Recognizing As Wins' to make the falsifiability-vs-restatement distinction explicit.

holes_found.description: The Overview hero thesis (docs/index.html:353) states 'None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain' but the verdict architecture contradicts this. 12 WINs are Refuted by Data — meaning they produced dome-specific quantitative results (11.78 Hz, κ=1.67 nT/μGal, etc.) that standard physics does not predict, and the data refuted those values. The honest formulation conflates 'non-discriminating' (Std Model Explains) with 'discriminating but wrong' (Refuted by Data) — two structurally distinct failure modes the review elsewhere carefully separates (Verdict Assignment Criteria section devotes ~200 words to the distinction).
holes_found.recommendation: Reword the hero thesis to 'None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that survives both internal-consistency and measurement checks.' This preserves the rhetorical force (the review still finds 0 dome wins) while matching the verdict architecture. Alternative: split the hero into two clauses — '18 are non-discriminating with standard physics; the remaining 52 produce dome-specific results that fail either internal-consistency or data checks.' The latter is more honest but less punchy; the operator should choose based on overview-skim priority.

holes_found.description: The stance statement opener (docs/index.html:341, 'The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology.') sits in direct tension with Principle 3 of the Evaluation Guide ('Mainstream physics is not assumed correct by default'). A dome defender can quote both verbatim and assert the review violates its own methodological commitment in its overview opening. Already partially flagged in HOLISTIC-HOL-NARRATIVE.c2 (2026-05-28); flagging here for stress-test-specific framing.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) rewrite Principle 3 to acknowledge that the review reaches a conclusion (mainstream physics IS correct on this specific question) which it now treats as established rather than open — 'We did not assume globe correctness at the start; we conclude it from the evidence reviewed below,' or (b) move the stance statement below the verdict bars and falsifiability module so that the conclusion appears AFTER the structural evidence rather than before it. The current order — conclusion first, evidence after — invites the procedural objection regardless of the truth-value of the conclusion.

holes_found.description: The 89.7% honest-accuracy denominator (docs/index.html accuracy-math-section: '70 confirmed ÷ (70 + 4 acknowledged + 4 silent)') uses a 'silent failures' count without a stated operational definition on the overview. The card asserts the dome's 94.5% is 'manually entered with a self-serving denominator' while not surfacing the criteria by which the review chose 4 silent failures rather than 3 or 7. A dome defender can argue the review constructs its own denominator with the same methodological freedom it criticizes the dome for using.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a brief tooltip or sublabel to the '4 silent failures' cell on the scorecard that names the operational definition: e.g., 'Predictions whose stated window-close date has passed by 14+ days with no result posted on the dome's page.' Link to the p3-failures section's per-PRED justification. The definition does not need to be lengthy; it needs to exist on the overview so the denominator can be audited without clicking through.

holes_found.description: No Overview-level acknowledgment of the review-as-living-document framing. A dome defender will note the curmudgeon's six holistic reruns in six days alongside the dome's 41-day manual-edit drought, framing the review as 'constantly finding errors in itself' while the model is 'stable.' This is logically weak (adversarial self-review is intentional) but rhetorically available to casual readers.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: add a single sentence to the methodology paragraph in the Overview noting that the review is maintained by adversarial AI agents that continuously surface errors against the document, and that this self-correction is a feature, not a sign of instability. Risk: this acknowledgment opens its own attack surface (the 'AI reviewing AI' tu quoque from c1 secondary rebuttal 2). Operator should weigh whether explicit acknowledgment helps more than it hurts.

stronger_arguments.description: Add a worked example to the eg-falsifiability section showing what a WIN satisfying Condition 1 would look like. Use a fictional scenario: 'If the dome geometry produced a Schumann fundamental of 22.6 Hz (which the bare dome cavity would predict) AND independent measurements showed Earth's actual Schumann resonance was 22.6 Hz, that would be Condition 1 met — discriminating, derived, confirmed. The reason this scenario doesn't apply is that observed Schumann is 7.83 Hz, requiring the dome to import the globe radius to match.' This demonstrates the conditions can in principle be satisfied independent of revising existing verdicts, neutralizing the tautology critique with a concrete template.
sources: Internal — derived from this stress test analysis

stronger_arguments.description: Add a verdict category 'Genuine Prediction (pending)' or 'Awaiting Test' as a placeholder for in-flight discriminating predictions like the August 2026 eclipse. The category would currently contain WIN-010 (or PRED-eclipse-001) marked as pending. The non-empty success-adjacent category defeats the c1 'no success node' critique architecturally rather than only via prose ('On the absence of a Confirmed category'). After August 2026, the category either gains entries (if the eclipse hits the dome-specific range) or empties back out.
sources: Internal — extends the c1 falsifiability framework recommendation

stronger_arguments.description: Reframe the falsifiability module heading from 'What Would Change Our Verdict' to 'Operational Falsifiability Conditions' with a sublabel: 'Concrete tests pre-committed to before the data; we will recognize a dome win if any of these conditions is met after the future test resolves.' This shifts the rhetorical weight from 'we promise' to 'we have set the test gates'; the dome defender's tautology critique applies less cleanly to test gates than to verdict-mirroring conditions.
sources: Internal — rhetorical restructuring of the c1 ds-falsifiability-module

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome defender's c2 tautology critique contains a genuine methodological observation: the c1 fix (eg-falsifiability section + ds-falsifiability-module) was applied at the prose layer but not the architecture layer. The four falsifiability conditions ARE in fact tightly coupled to the existing verdict criteria — Condition 1 is 'the inverse of Std Model Explains,' Condition 3 restates the criterion that makes the cavity-frequency cluster Self-Contradicted, Condition 4 restates the criterion that makes the coordinate-system WINs Misleading. A more architecturally-grounded fix would have either (a) added a Genuine Prediction (pending) verdict category as a placeholder for in-flight tests, or (b) introduced falsifiability conditions that name future measurements rather than restate existing classification rules. The dome defender is correct that prose-layer falsifiability does not equal architecture-layer falsifiability.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing that the c1 fix is prose-deep does not validate the dome. Even if the falsifiability module is rhetorically vulnerable, the underlying verdicts remain correct — every individual WIN still produces results that either fail internal-consistency, are refuted by data, or do not discriminate from standard physics. The architecture-vs-prose gap is a presentation problem, not a verdict problem. The dome defender's critique is best understood as 'your module is rhetorically vulnerable' rather than 'your verdicts are wrong' — the latter would require attacking the individual analyses, which the c1 stress test composite defense conceded the dome cannot do successfully. The fix is to make the module more architecturally robust (Stronger Arguments 1-3), not to abandon any verdict.

text_fingerprint.review_subject: Overview tab + eg-falsifiability + ds-falsifiability-module + eg-verdicts + stance statement + accuracy math card
text_fingerprint.overview_thesis_text: None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.

summary_for_decider: c2 stress test finds c1 mostly addressed (4 of 6 holes fixed, 2 partial), composite-c1-defense substantially weakened by eclipse pre-commitment. Strongest fresh c2 rebuttal: ds-falsifiability-module on Overview is tautologically coupled to the existing verdict criteria — three of its four conditions reformulate Self-Contradicted/Misleading criteria, and the four-badge zero stack visually doubles the c1 'rigged framework' optics. Major hole #2: hero thesis 'no dome-specific result standard physics cannot already explain' contradicts the 12 Refuted-by-Data WINs which ARE dome-specific. Three Stronger Arguments propose architecture-level fixes (worked Condition-1 example; Genuine Prediction (pending) verdict category placeholder; rename module to 'Operational Falsifiability Conditions'). HOLISTIC-HOL-TAXONOMY.c2 reached the same architectural conclusion from the inside-out lens; consider grouping these into a single EXP rather than per-hole patches. recommended_action: minor_edit on the two major framing items (hero wording, module sublabel); the architecture-level recommendations (verdict category placeholder, module rename) are EXP-scale.
deeper_analysis:
The c1 stress test (April 2026) identified six holes, of which four are now substantively fixed (success-category framing, Std Model Explains nuance, scorecard framing, eclipse pre-commitment), one is partially fixed (asymmetric-burden acknowledgment in Part 8 but not the Evaluation Guide preface), and one (medicine analogy alternative for anti-establishment readers) is not addressed. The composite c1 dome defense (taxonomy critique + eclipse 'wait and see') is now structurally weaker because the eclipse pre-commitment closes the wait-and-see strategy.

The c2 stress test finds the dome defender's strongest available attack has moved one layer up: from 'your taxonomy has no success category' (c1) to 'the section you added to address the no-success-category critique restates the existing verdicts in different language.' This is a narrower critique than c1 (7.5/10 vs 8.5/10) but it is structurally accurate: the ds-falsifiability-module's four conditions are tightly coupled to the existing verdict criteria, and the four-badge zero stack visually doubles the c1 'rigged framework' optics rather than blunting them.

A secondary c2 finding is the hero-text overstatement: 'None of the 70 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain' conflates Std Model Explains (non-discriminating) with Refuted by Data (discriminating but wrong) — two failure modes the Verdict Assignment Criteria section carefully separates. The dome defender can quote the hero verbatim and contrast it with the verdict bars below to argue the review's rhetoric contradicts its own architecture.

The stance statement and 89.7% honest-accuracy attacks are real but moderate (already partially flagged in HOLISTIC-HOL-NARRATIVE.c2 and benefiting from inline links to the p3-failures justifications respectively). The methodology-churn-optics attack is logically weak but rhetorically available to casual readers.

The recommended fixes are architectural rather than purely prose-layer: add a worked example to eg-falsifiability showing what Condition 1 satisfaction looks like; consider adding a Genuine Prediction (pending) verdict category as a placeholder for in-flight tests; reword the hero thesis to match the verdict architecture; either rewrite Principle 3 to acknowledge the stance statement or move the stance statement below the structural evidence. None of these changes weakens any individual verdict — they close presentation gaps that the c2 dome defender's composite defense exploits.

Note on holistic interaction: HOLISTIC-HOL-TAXONOMY.c2 (2026-05-29) reached the same conclusion via the taxonomy-application-consistency lens — it explicitly noted that the 'rigged taxonomy / no-positive-category objection' is addressed by the Verdict Assignment Criteria section, while flagging fresh holes around Self-Contradicted cluster inflation. The c2 stress test extends this finding by noting the SAME architectural gap surfaces differently when read from the dome-defender's outside-in perspective vs. the taxonomy-coherence inside-out perspective. Both reviews recommend prose-only changes; neither has flagged the architecture-level falsifiability-conditions and verdict-category fixes proposed here.
